name: PR-stack Review Response (round 1: PR #20) -- truth-sync fixes + debt registration

design_notes.what_this_feature_is: Feature 046 is a review-response overhead feature (not a handover deliverable): it answers CodeRabbit's six actionable comments on PR #20 (CHANGES_REQUESTED) as a single new commit at the stack tip (stacked-PR convention). Per the planner's AH-2 triage (.agent/planning_notes_046.md), the six findings resolve into three dispositions: (1) TWO live truth-sync defects, both confirmed still present at HEAD ada3b9c, get FIXED: .agent/product_spec_005.md's frontmatter status (draft, contradicting its own 'Status: ACTIVE' body and the already-active manifest) flips to active; docs/planning/ROADMAP.md's stale Sequencing Recommendation sentence (still calling Track 2 the phase-005 selection, contradicting the file's OWN supersession note) is rewritten. (2) TWO items get REGISTERED AS DEBT (no code change): NDEBT-019 (Low, the pipefail-less validate.sh verification-command authoring pattern) and NDEBT-020 (Medium, the 045-review evaluator's self-caught/self-restored rm -f incident against real CHANGELOG.md). (3) THREE items are EXPLAIN-AND-RESOLVE (no repository change at all -- the orchestrator posts the planner's pre-written rationale as PR reply comments; this contract's scope of change does not include them). This contract covers EXACTLY the planner's scope: three files, .agent/product_spec_005.md, docs/planning/ROADMAP.md, docs/planning/DEBT.md -- nothing else.
design_notes.payload_literal_discrepancy: feature_list_005.json's own acceptance test for feature 046 (item 15, mirroring feature 045's own acceptance test 15) asserts `grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: 10 passed, 0 failed'` against `bash tools/validate.sh --payload`'s output. Live-verified against the REAL, current, unmodified tools/validate.sh (which this contract does not touch): the actual payload-mode SUMMARY line is `SUMMARY (payload mode): 10 passed, 0 failed` -- the literal in the feature list is missing the `(payload mode) ` infix and, run with `-x` (exact whole-line match), can NEVER match the real, unchanging output. This is a textual discrepancy in the feature list's OWN acceptance-test wording (not something 046 is scoped to fix -- 'no feature_list edits beyond what the planner already made' is an explicit non-goal), and including the exact broken literal verbatim would make this contract's own verification entry 21 permanently unsatisfiable for any implementation, correct or not -- precisely the round-0 HIGH-severity defect class contract 045 was rejected for and had to fix (an unsatisfiable acceptance test). Verification entry 21 therefore asserts the CORRECT, real, unchanging payload-mode output (`SUMMARY (payload mode): 10 passed, 0 failed`) with the same exit-status-assertion discipline the feature list's own pattern models, rather than reproducing a knowingly-false string. Flagged here explicitly for validator/evaluator visibility and disposition at Mode A review; entry 20 (the default-sweep companion) has no such discrepancy and is retained 100% verbatim.
design_notes.head_caret_timing_note: The planner's own acceptance tests for the ROADMAP anti-vacuity companion (entry 6) and the scope guard (entry 15) are written as `git show HEAD^:...` / `git diff --name-only HEAD^ HEAD -- ...`, anticipating execution AFTER the orchestrator commits this feature's changes as a new tip commit (at which point HEAD = the 046 commit and HEAD^ = ada3b9c, the pre-046 tip). At CONTRACT-PROPOSAL and IMPLEMENTATION time (this dispatch), no such commit exists yet -- HEAD is still ada3b9c and HEAD^ resolves to 422a074 (feature 044's own commit), so entry 6's literal is evaluated against a DIFFERENT, earlier commit pair than it will be at Mode B/QA time; it happens to still PASS now (the stale sentence was already present at 422a074 too, confirmed live), but this is coincidental, not the check's real load-bearing window. Entry 15's mandated `HEAD^ HEAD` scope-guard literal is CURRENTLY MISLEADING pre-commit: run today it captures feature 045's OWN five-file diff window (CHANGELOG.md/NIZAM.json/docs/guide/index.html/CONTEXT.md/README.md, none of which are in 046's exclude-list), which is irrelevant to 046's own scope and would incorrectly report non-empty -- NOT a genuine 046 scope violation. This contract's real, load-bearing, pre-commit scope proof is entry 15's own `vlib_scope_guard` invocation (working-tree-anchored against the current HEAD, correctly .agent-excluding by design, dry-run-proven clean); the mandated HEAD-caret literal is retained (as a comment/note in entry 15, not a hard gate at this stage) purely so it is honored once the orchestrator's later commit makes it meaningful, per the same treatment contract 045 gave its own timing-deferred mandated literals.
design_notes.planner_feature_list_edit_tolerance: The current working tree already carries an UNCOMMITTED, pre-existing modification to `.agent/feature_list_005.json` (the planner's own addition of the feature-046 entry, made before this contract was authored) plus a new untracked `.agent/planning_notes_046.md` (this contract's own source material). Neither is part of this contract's scope of change (per the explicit non-goal 'no feature_list edits beyond what the planner already made') and neither should be flagged as a scope violation. The .agent immutability guard (entry 14) and scope guard (entry 15) are both designed to TOLERATE these two specific, already-existing, non-046-attributable paths while still catching any OTHER unauthorized .agent modification or any out-of-scope shipped-file change -- dry-run-proven against the real repository (which currently carries exactly this pre-existing state) and, for the immutability guard's adversarial direction, against a disposable scratch git repository.
design_notes.embedded_pipe_defect_discovery: Live-discovered during this contract's own dry-run process (methodology/02's mandated defect catalogue applied to the generator's OWN authored checks, not just the shipped deliverables): DEBT.md's existing row convention (see NDEBT-016's own row, which contains the unescaped regex `(?:^|_)(?:neg|invalid)(?:_|\.)`) legitimately embeds literal `|` characters inside backtick code spans -- valid GFM (GitHub renders pipes inside code spans literally, without needing escaping, inside a table cell) and consistent with this contract's own NDEBT-019 row (which necessarily quotes the shell pipeline `bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep ...` verbatim). A first-draft version of verification entry 13 (DEBT.md row content substantive check) used a naive 4-column-position regex (`\|[^|]*\|[^|]*\|[^|]*\|([^|]*)\|`) to isolate the Description column specifically -- this BROKE on the real NDEBT-019 row's own embedded pipes, prematurely truncating the captured group before reaching the required 'pipefail' token, and produced a FALSE FAIL on a genuinely compliant fixture (caught live during this contract's own dry-run, never shipped). FIX: entry 13 was rebuilt to match against the WHOLE row line (identified by its `^\| *NDEBT-0XX *\|` prefix), checking for the required tokens anywhere in that line rather than attempting column-position isolation -- sacrificing column-precision for correctness, since the anti-vacuity property that matters (the token appears on THIS row's line, not merely somewhere else in the file) is fully preserved. Re-proven against both the pre-implementation fixture (correctly fails, row absent) and the fixed post-implementation fixture (correctly passes).
design_notes.roadmap_sentence_scoping_defect_discover: A SEPARATE, related self-inflicted defect was also caught live during dry-run: the FIRST DRAFT of this contract's own planned ROADMAP.md change_log entry (documenting the sentence rewrite) quoted the exact stale sentence text verbatim inside its own summary prose ('rewrote ... the stale ... sentence (\'Track 2 is the recommended phase 005\') ...') -- this made verification entry 5 (which greps the WHOLE FILE for that exact stale phrase) falsely FAIL on a fully compliant post-implementation fixture, since the phrase now reappeared, quoted, in the new change_log entry describing its own removal. FIX: the change_log summary text was rewritten to describe the fix without reproducing the literal banned phrase (paraphrased as 'which had wrongly continued to call Track 2 the phase-005 selection'). This is the exact scope.files_modify[1]-authoring content the generator's own implementation step must follow verbatim to avoid re-introducing this defect.
design_notes.roadmap_and_spec_and_debt_version_bumps: Following the house release-metadata/documentation-truth convention each prior planning-document edit in this repository already establishes (CONTEXT.md's own v0.3.0 docs/-addition entry; ROADMAP.md's own v0.2.0/v0.3.0/v0.4.0 dated entries; DEBT.md's own undocumented-but-consistent version increments): product_spec_005.md's frontmatter `version` bumps 1.0.0 -> 1.1.0 with one new change_log entry (dated 2026-07-18, describing the status flip only -- the spec's OTHER frontmatter field `spec_version` is a distinct field representing the spec-format/methodology version, not the document's own revision, and is NOT touched, matching the planner's 'do NOT edit ... any other frontmatter field' constraint); ROADMAP.md's frontmatter `version` bumps 0.4.0 -> 0.5.0 with one new change_log entry; DEBT.md's frontmatter `version` bumps 0.11.0 -> 0.12.0 (DEBT.md carries no `change_log` array in its own frontmatter historically -- confirmed live, its schema treats `change_log` as optional -- so no change_log entry is added there, only the version bump the planner's own instruction and the feature list's own acceptance test require).
design_notes.estimated_lines_derivation: product_spec_005.md: 2 frontmatter lines changed (status, version) + 1 new change_log entry (~3 lines) = ~5. ROADMAP.md: 1 sentence rewritten (~2 lines, same paragraph) + 1 version line changed + 1 new change_log entry (~3 lines) = ~6. DEBT.md: 1 version line changed + 2 new table rows (~2 lines, each a single long physical line matching the existing house convention) = ~3. Total ~14 lines of genuine content change -- well under feature_list_005.json's own 60-line a-priori estimate, since this is a narrowly-scoped, single-sentence-plus-two-frontmatter-flips-plus-two-debt-rows fix, not new capability work. Per-feature scope-budget check: rolling average of the last 3 completed features (043=116, 044=320, 045=~77 tracked-diff lines per its own committed ledger) = ~171, 3x = ~513; ~14 is far under -- no per-feature flag. Cumulative check: scope_budget.total_lines_changed through 045 was last recorded at 3445 pre-045; +~77 (045's actual) +~14 (046's estimate) keeps cumulative well within the 3500 base / 4550 ceiling -- no cumulative-ceiling concern.
design_notes.dry_run_proof_summary:
All of the following were proven WITHOUT creating, editing, or deleting anything in the real repository tree at /home/cnross/workspace02/nizam-framework, other than this contract file itself: (1) `git show HEAD:...` (read-only) confirmed the anti-vacuity precondition for all three files (entry 1) and the mandated HEAD-caret literal's current, coincidental truth (entry 6). (2) A disposable scratch copy of the three real files was mutated to simulate the exact planned implementation (status flip + change_log entries; the ROADMAP sentence rewrite; the two new DEBT rows), and every mandated-literal and this-contract's-own verification command (entries 2-13, 20-21 logic) was re-run against BOTH the untouched pre-implementation copy (correctly FAILS on every gate-dependent entry) and the compliant post-implementation copy (correctly PASSES all of them) -- including two adversarial catches during this very process: the embedded-pipe column-regex defect (entry 13, see design_notes.embedded_pipe_defect_discovery) and the self-quoting stale-phrase defect in the generator's own planned change_log text (entry 5, see design_notes.roadmap_sentence_scoping_defect_discovery), both discovered live and fixed before being included here. (3) The Sequencing-Recommendation content-consistency check (entry 9) was proven correct in both directions using a line-join extraction (not a per-line grep), deliberately avoiding the NDEBT-014 item-3 line-wrap-fragility class. (4) The .agent immutability guard (entry 14) was proven against the REAL current tree (correctly tolerates the planner's own pre-existing feature_list_005.json edit and planning_notes_046.md) AND, per the revision-1 fix (see design_notes.entry14_fix), fully exercised in BOTH directions against a disposable scratch git repository: (i) the COMPLIANT post-implementation fixture -- .agent/product_spec_005.md itself modified (status draft->active) alongside the pre-existing .agent/feature_list_005.json edit, plus the new untracked .agent/contracts/046.json and .agent/planning_notes_046.md -- correctly PASSES; (ii) an UNAUTHORIZED tracked .agent edit (a mutated .agent/contracts/044.json, simulating an immutable-audit-record tamper) alongside that same compliant state correctly FAILS ('out-of-scope tracked .agent modification: .agent/contracts/044.json'), and reverting it restores a clean PASS. A stray untracked .agent file outside the allowed glob is separately confirmed caught (unchanged from the revision-0 proof). (5) The scope guard (entry 15)'s `vlib_scope_guard` component was proven clean against the real, current tree, and its adversarial direction was independently proven against a second disposable scratch git repository (a CHANGELOG.md-analog out-of-scope edit is correctly caught). (6) The two cross-feature invariants (entries 18-19, reused verbatim from contract 045's own entries 17-18) were re-run against the real tree (currently green) and against the compliant post-implementation DEBT.md fixture (still green, confirming NDEBT-019/020's Low/Medium severities do not regress the zero-P0/P1 invariant) and against a deliberately-mis-severitied NDEBT-019 fixture (High instead of Low), which correctly FAILS both the row-registration literal (entry 10) and the P0/P1 scan (entry 19). (7) The two compliance-sweep entries (20-21) were re-run live against the real, current, unmodified tree and both currently PASS (validate.sh is not touched by this contract, so no regression is expected or introduced). Nothing under the real repository tree was created, edited, or deleted to produce any of these proofs, other than this contract file.

REVISION 2 ADDITIONS, per @evaluator round-1 findings (all re-proven in the generator's own scratchpad, never touching the real repository tree, other than read-only `git show HEAD:...` reads): (8) the evaluator's exact meaning-reversal fixture ('Track 2 is NOT candidate scope for a subsequent phase; it remains the recommended selection for phase 005 going forward, activated through the standard planning lifecycle.') was independently reproduced and, against the revision-1 commands, confirmed to falsely PASS both entry 5 and entry 9 -- then, against the rebuilt entry 9, correctly FAILS (the pinned exact sentence is absent). (9) A second, 'belt-and-suspenders' adversarial fixture -- containing BOTH the genuine pinned sentence AND a separate, additional sentence re-asserting 'Track 2 is still... the recommended path' elsewhere in the same section -- was run against the rebuilt entry 9 and correctly FAILS on the remainder co-occurrence guard specifically (not the pinned-sentence assertion, which passes), proving that second layer is independently load-bearing. (10) The fully compliant post-implementation ROADMAP.md fixture (containing the exact pinned sentence and nothing else altered) correctly PASSES the rebuilt entry 9. (11) The real, current, pre-implementation ROADMAP.md was re-run against the rebuilt entry 9 (read-only) and still correctly FAILS, confirming no regression to the gate-dependent pattern. (12) The new entry 22 (row-count-delta) was proven in a disposable scratch git repository across three states: pre-implementation (delta=0) correctly FAILS; the compliant two-new-row state (delta=2) correctly PASSES; an unauthorized third-row state (NDEBT-019/020/021, delta=3) correctly FAILS; the same check was also re-run read-only against the real repository's current state and correctly FAILS (delta=0, pre-implementation). (13) Entry 14's two required polarities (from the revision-1 fix) were re-run once more in a fresh scratch git repository to confirm no regression from this revision's unrelated changes: the compliant post-implementation state still PASSES; the unauthorized .agent/contracts/044.json tamper still FAILS. Nothing under the real repository tree at /home/cnross/workspace02/nizam-framework was created, edited, or deleted to produce any of these revision-2 proofs, other than this contract file itself; no git tag or sign-off of any kind was created anywhere in this revision's proof process.
design_notes.entry14_fix: REVISED at revision 1 per Mode A's round-0 BLOCKER finding: entry 14's revision-0 tracked-diff whitelist tolerated ONLY .agent/feature_list_005.json (the planner's pre-existing edit), but never .agent/product_spec_005.md -- this contract's OWN PRIMARY deliverable (scope.files_modify[0]), which every correct implementation of entries 2/3/4 MUST modify (the status flip, version bump, and change_log growth all land in that same tracked file). As written, entry 14 was therefore internally contradictory and unsatisfiable by ANY correct implementation -- the identical unsatisfiable-acceptance-test defect class contract 045 was rejected for at its own round 0. Mode A proved this empirically in a scratch repository: post-implementation, entry 14 exited 1 with 'out-of-scope tracked .agent modification: .agent/product_spec_005.md'. The revision-0 dry_run_proof_summary(4) claim that the guard was 'proven both directions' was itself an unsupported claim -- it never actually exercised a fixture where the contract's own spec deliverable was modified, only the planner's pre-existing feature_list_005.json edit. FIX (this revision): added '.agent/product_spec_005.md' to entry 14's tracked-diff tolerated case (now: `.agent/feature_list_005.json|.agent/product_spec_005.md) ;;`). Re-proven in a fresh disposable scratch git repository, BOTH required polarities: (i) the compliant post-implementation state (product_spec_005.md AND feature_list_005.json both tracked-modified, plus the new untracked contracts/046.json and planning_notes_046.md) now correctly PASSES; (ii) an unauthorized tracked .agent edit (a mutated .agent/contracts/044.json, simulating tampering with an immutable audit record) alongside that same compliant state correctly FAILS, and reverting it restores PASS -- confirming the guard remains genuinely load-bearing (not weakened into a no-op) even after tolerating the spec deliverable. docs/planning/ROADMAP.md and docs/planning/DEBT.md are NOT .agent/ paths, so their own modifications were never affected by this guard or this fix -- they are governed solely by entry 15's scope guard, unchanged.
design_notes.paraphrase_bypass_fix: REVISED at revision 2 per @evaluator round-1 HIGH finding: revision 1's entry 9 (and the feature-list's own mandated entry 5 literal) checked for a bag of independent tokens ('candidate scope' present, 'subsequent phase' present, 'recommended phase 005' absent) rather than the section's actual semantic meaning -- exactly the NDEBT-014 item-5 lesson ('natural-language content bounds... cannot stop paraphrase mirroring -- only closed-set shapes with exact-text pinning proved airtight'). The evaluator demonstrated a single, realistic paraphrase that simultaneously bypasses BOTH checks while reversing the required meaning: 'Track 2 is NOT candidate scope for a subsequent phase; it remains the recommended selection for phase 005 going forward, activated through the standard planning lifecycle.' -- it contains both required bag-of-tokens ('candidate scope', 'subsequent phase', negated) and dodges the banned substring ('recommended phase 005') via 'recommended selection for phase 005'. Independently reproduced live: this exact fixture passes both the revision-1 entry 5 and entry 9 commands. FIX (this revision, the strongest closed-set form, since the generator authors this exact replacement sentence, mirroring the 045 checklist-SHA precedent of pinning content the generator itself controls): (a) scope.files_modify[1].purpose now specifies the EXACT replacement sentence text verbatim (see below); (b) entry 9 is rebuilt to require that EXACT sentence, contiguous and verbatim (line-join-tolerant, since it may wrap across source lines), as its primary positive assertion, replacing the two independent bag-of-token asserts; (c) as a second, independent layer (defense-in-depth, per the evaluator's 'AND/OR' suggestion, choosing both): after removing the one pinned sentence occurrence from the section's line-joined text, the REMAINDER must not simultaneously contain both 'Track 2' and 'recommended' -- structurally catching any additional sentence that re-asserts the stale meaning even if the pinned sentence is also genuinely present. Re-proven: (i) the evaluator's exact meaning-reversal fixture now FAILS (the pinned sentence is simply absent, since the paraphrase is a different sentence entirely); (ii) a 'belt-and-suspenders' adversarial fixture containing BOTH the pinned sentence AND a second, separate sentence re-asserting 'Track 2 is still... the recommended path' is independently caught by layer (c) alone, proving that layer is genuinely load-bearing and not merely redundant with (b); (iii) the compliant, fully-implemented fixture PASSES; (iv) the real, current, pre-implementation ROADMAP.md still correctly FAILS (the pinned sentence does not exist there yet). Entry 5 (the feature-list's own mandated literal) is left unmodified verbatim, per the established house convention of retaining a mandated-but-limited literal check while pairing it with a genuinely load-bearing companion (the exact pattern contract 045's entry 3 established) -- entry 9 is now that companion, and the overall verification suite's soundness on this deliverable no longer depends on entry 5 alone.
design_notes.row_count_delta_fix: NEW at revision 2 per @evaluator round-1 LOW finding: entry 10's closed-set ID check (`set(rows)=={'NDEBT-019','NDEBT-020'}`) only ever captures rows whose ID literally matches one of the two expected IDs -- it structurally cannot detect an ADDITIONAL, unauthorized third row (e.g. a stray NDEBT-021) added alongside the two authorized ones, since such a row simply never enters the regex's own capture set. Independently reproduced live: a fixture with NDEBT-019 (Low), NDEBT-020 (Medium), AND an unauthorized NDEBT-021 (Low) row still passes entry 10 verbatim. FIX: a new, HEAD-anchored companion verification entry (appended at the end of the array as entry 22, not inserted mid-array, so entries 1-21's indices and every existing cross-reference to them stay stable) asserts the total count of '| NDEBT-' rows in the working tree is exactly 2 more than the count at HEAD -- a lightweight row-count-delta bound, not a full-file SHA pin (DEBT.md is a living, cross-feature document shared by every future feature's own debt registrations, unlike 045's single-feature-owned checklist, so a byte-exact pin would be the wrong, over-rigid tool here, per the evaluator's own observation). Re-proven in a disposable scratch git repository across three states: (i) pre-implementation (delta=0) correctly FAILS; (ii) the compliant two-new-row state (delta=2) correctly PASSES; (iii) the unauthorized third-row state (delta=3) correctly FAILS.

files_modify.purpose: Flip frontmatter `status` from `draft` to `active` (correcting the truth-sync defect against the body's own 'Status: ACTIVE' declaration and the already-active manifest phase-005 entry); bump frontmatter `version` 1.0.0 -> 1.1.0; append exactly one new `change_log` entry (dated 2026-07-18) describing the status flip. No other frontmatter field (`spec_version`, `last_audited`, `authoritative_source`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, `tags`, `id`, `title`, `description`) and no body text is touched.

Rewrite exactly the one stale Sequencing Recommendation sentence ('Track 2 is the recommended phase 005, activated through the standard planning lifecycle.') to the following EXACT, pinned replacement text (verbatim -- REVISED at revision 2 per design_notes.paraphrase_bypass_fix, closed-set exact-text pinned since the generator authors this sentence, mirroring 045's checklist-content pin): 'Track 2 was superseded as the phase-005 selection by NIP-0001 (see the supersession note above) and remains candidate scope for a subsequent phase.' -- consistent with the file's own existing Track 2 supersession note (~line 95-102); bump frontmatter `version` 0.4.0 -> 0.5.0; append exactly one new `change_log` entry (dated 2026-07-18) describing the fix WITHOUT quoting the stale phrase verbatim (per design_notes.roadmap_sentence_scoping_defect_discovery). No other ROADMAP.md sentence, section, or line is edited.

Add exactly two new rows to the Open table -- NDEBT-019 (Low, the pipefail-less validate.sh verification-command authoring pattern) and NDEBT-020 (Medium, the 045-review evaluator's self-caught/self-restored rm -f incident plus the strengthened probe-isolation rule) -- in the house row format (`| ID | Date | Severity | Description | Remediation |`), each a single physical line; bump frontmatter `version` 0.11.0 -> 0.12.0. DEBT.md carries no `change_log` array in its frontmatter (confirmed absent, schema-optional), so none is added. No existing row is edited, reordered, or removed.

non_goals: No edit to any `.agent/evidence/*` file -- immutable point-in-time audit captures, including (per the NDEBT-019 disposition) the historical evidence files the pipefail-less pattern appears in.

No edit to `.agent/evidence/045-release-readiness-checklist.md` -- SHA-pinned by contract 045's own verification entry 17; byte-identity must survive this feature untouched (re-verified live by this contract's own entry 18).

No edit to any `methodology/*` file -- NDEBT-019's hardening (pipefail/exit-code discipline in the verification-authoring standard) is explicitly deferred to a future self-compliance phase, not hot-fixed here.
No edit to `NIZAM.json`, any `README*` file, or `CHANGELOG.md`.
No edit to `.agent/run_state.json` -- orchestrator-owned (re-verified live by this contract's own entry 16).

No edit to `v0.6.0-release-notes.md` -- pre-existing untracked file, content hash must remain 15ac6acaa9a549a171b5f49617654cac189322fbab760d78104b4c3f4662c00b (re-verified live by this contract's own entry 17).

No edit to `.agent/feature_list_005.json` beyond what the planner has already made -- this contract's implementation step does not touch it at all (the planner's own pre-existing edit is tolerated, not authored by this contract).
No edit to any `.agent/contracts/*.json` other than this `046.json`, or any `.agent/qa/*.json` -- immutable audit records.

No git commit, push, PR creation, PR merge, or branch operation of any kind under this contract or its implementation step -- landing the tip commit is a separate, later pipeline action, per the stacked-PR convention (one new commit at the stack tip; earlier PR threads resolved by reference to it).

No repository change of any kind for the three EXPLAIN-AND-RESOLVE findings (the `.agent/evidence/031-verify-04.txt` looseness comment, the feature_list acceptance-checks-not-load-bearing comment (REFUTED), and the ecosystem/README.md-stub comment) -- their resolution is the planner's pre-written PR-reply rationale (.agent/planning_notes_046.md Sec 2), posted by the orchestrator, not a code or document change.
No creation of any new schema, protocol document, template, or CLI tool -- this feature is documentation truth-sync plus debt registration only.
No remediation of any OTHER open docs/planning/DEBT.md item -- this contract registers two NEW rows only, it does not touch or resolve any existing row.

verification.acceptance_test: ANTI-VACUITY (HEAD-anchored, current tip ada3b9c): none of this contract's deliverables already exist -- spec status is still 'draft', ROADMAP's stale Sequencing Recommendation sentence is still present, ROADMAP change_log still has exactly 4 entries, DEBT.md version is still 0.11.0, and neither NDEBT-019 nor NDEBT-020 is registered.
SPEC STATUS FLIP (feature_list_005.json's own mandated literal, run verbatim): frontmatter status is 'active' and change_log grew to >= 2 entries.
verification.command: python3 -c "import yaml,sys; d=yaml.safe_load(open('.agent/product_spec_005.md',encoding='utf-8').read().split('---')[1]); sys.exit(0 if d['status']=='active' and len(d.get('change_log',[]))>=2 else 1)" && echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: SPEC FRONTMATTER SCHEMA VALIDATION (feature_list_005.json's own mandated literal, run verbatim): the spec's frontmatter still validates against schema/frontmatter.schema.json.
verification.command: python3 -c "import json,yaml,jsonschema; d=open('.agent/product_spec_005.md',encoding='utf-8').read().split('---')[1]; jsonschema.validate(yaml.safe_load(d), json.load(open('schema/frontmatter.schema.json')))" && echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: SPEC BODY + OTHER-FRONTMATTER UNCHANGED invariant (this contract's own addition, per the planner's 'do NOT edit the spec body or any other frontmatter field' constraint): the body text is byte-identical to HEAD, and every frontmatter key other than status/version/change_log is unchanged.
verification.command:
python3 -c "
import subprocess, yaml
head_text = subprocess.check_output(['git', 'show', 'HEAD:.agent/product_spec_005.md']).decode()
work_text = open('.agent/product_spec_005.md', encoding='utf-8').read()
head_body = head_text.split('---', 2)[2]
work_body = work_text.split('---', 2)[2]
assert head_body == work_body, 'spec body changed -- forbidden'
head_fm = yaml.safe_load(head_text.split('---')[1])
work_fm = yaml.safe_load(work_text.split('---')[1])
allowed = {'status', 'version', 'change_log'}
for k in set(head_fm) | set(work_fm):
    if k in allowed:
        continue
    assert head_fm.get(k) == work_fm.get(k), f'frontmatter field {k} changed -- forbidden'
print('PASS')
"

verification.acceptance_test: ROADMAP STALE SENTENCE REMOVED (feature_list_005.json's own mandated literal, run verbatim): the stale 'Track 2 is the recommended phase 005' sentence no longer appears anywhere in ROADMAP.md.
verification.command: grep -Eq 'Track 2 is the recommended phase 005' docs/planning/ROADMAP.md && { echo 'stale sentence still present'; exit 1; } || echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: ROADMAP ANTI-VACUITY -- MANDATED HEAD-CARET LITERAL (feature_list_005.json's own mandated literal, run verbatim; see design_notes.head_caret_timing_note for the pre-commit timing caveat): the stale sentence DID exist on the parent commit.
verification.command: git show HEAD^:docs/planning/ROADMAP.md | grep -Eq 'Track 2 is the recommended phase 005' && echo PASS || { echo "mandated HEAD^ anti-vacuity literal did not match (note: pre-046-commit, HEAD^ resolves to feature 044's commit, not the pre-046 tip -- see design_notes for the timing caveat)"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ROADMAP CHANGE_LOG GROWTH (feature_list_005.json's own mandated literal, run verbatim): change_log grew from 4 to >= 5 entries.
verification.command: python3 -c "import yaml,sys; d=yaml.safe_load(open('docs/planning/ROADMAP.md',encoding='utf-8').read().split('---')[1]); sys.exit(0 if len(d.get('change_log',[]))>=5 else 1)" && echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: ROADMAP FRONTMATTER SCHEMA VALIDATION (this contract's own addition, cheap parity check): ROADMAP.md's frontmatter still validates against schema/frontmatter.schema.json.
verification.command: python3 -c "import json,yaml,jsonschema; d=open('docs/planning/ROADMAP.md',encoding='utf-8').read().split('---')[1]; jsonschema.validate(yaml.safe_load(d), json.load(open('schema/frontmatter.schema.json')))" && echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: ROADMAP SEQUENCING-RECOMMENDATION CONTENT CONSISTENCY (this contract's own addition; REVISED at revision 2 per @evaluator round-1 HIGH finding -- see design_notes.paraphrase_bypass_fix): the Sequencing Recommendation section must contain the EXACT, pinned replacement sentence verbatim (line-join-tolerant, closed-set exact-text pinning per the 040/045 precedent -- not a bag-of-independent-tokens check), the stale phrase must be absent, AND no OTHER sentence in the section may co-mention 'Track 2' and 'recommended' (a structural guard against a meaning-reversing paraphrase that dodges the banned substring while re-asserting the stale claim).
verification.command:
python3 -c "
lines = open('docs/planning/ROADMAP.md', encoding='utf-8').read().splitlines()
start = next(i for i, l in enumerate(lines) if l.strip() == '## Sequencing Recommendation')
end = next(i for i in range(start + 1, len(lines)) if lines[i].startswith('## '))
block = ' '.join(lines[start + 1:end])
pinned = 'Track 2 was superseded as the phase-005 selection by NIP-0001 (see the supersession note above) and remains candidate scope for a subsequent phase.'
assert pinned in block, 'exact pinned replacement sentence not found (verbatim, contiguous) in the Sequencing Recommendation section'
assert 'recommended phase 005' not in block, 'stale phrase still present in Sequencing Recommendation section'
remainder = block.replace(pinned, '', 1)
assert not ('Track 2' in remainder and 'recommended' in remainder), 'a sentence OTHER than the pinned one still co-mentions Track 2 and recommended -- possible meaning-reversing restatement'
print('PASS')
"

verification.acceptance_test: DEBT.md ROW REGISTRATION (feature_list_005.json's own mandated literal, run verbatim): exactly NDEBT-019 and NDEBT-020 are registered, both severity Low or Medium.
verification.command: python3 -c "import re,sys; t=open('docs/planning/DEBT.md',encoding='utf-8').read(); rows={m.group(1):m.group(2).strip() for m in re.finditer(r'\|\s*(NDEBT-0(?:19|20))\s*\|[^|]*\|\s*(Low|Medium|High|P0|P1)\s*\|', t)}; sys.exit(0 if set(rows)=={'NDEBT-019','NDEBT-020'} and all(v in ('Low','Medium') for v in rows.values()) else 1)" && echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: DEBT.md VERSION BUMP (feature_list_005.json's own mandated literal, run verbatim): frontmatter version changed from 0.11.0.
verification.command: python3 -c "import yaml,sys; d=yaml.safe_load(open('docs/planning/DEBT.md',encoding='utf-8').read().split('---')[1]); sys.exit(0 if str(d['version'])!='0.11.0' else 1)" && echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: DEBT.md FRONTMATTER SCHEMA VALIDATION (this contract's own addition, cheap parity check): DEBT.md's frontmatter still validates against schema/frontmatter.schema.json.
verification.command: python3 -c "import json,yaml,jsonschema; d=open('docs/planning/DEBT.md',encoding='utf-8').read().split('---')[1]; jsonschema.validate(yaml.safe_load(d), json.load(open('schema/frontmatter.schema.json')))" && echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: DEBT.md ROW CONTENT SUBSTANTIVE CHECK (this contract's own addition; whole-row-line matching, NOT column-position regex -- see design_notes.embedded_pipe_defect_discovery for why): NDEBT-019's row names pipefail/exit-code/exit-status; NDEBT-020's row names 'rm -f' and cwd/working-directory.
verification.command:
python3 -c "
import re
t = open('docs/planning/DEBT.md', encoding='utf-8').read()
lines = t.splitlines()
line19 = next((l for l in lines if re.match(r'^\| *NDEBT-019 *\|', l)), None)
assert line19, 'NDEBT-019 row not found'
assert ('pipefail' in line19) or ('exit code' in line19) or ('exit status' in line19) or ('exit-code' in line19), 'NDEBT-019 row missing pipefail/exit-code/exit-status mention'
line20 = next((l for l in lines if re.match(r'^\| *NDEBT-020 *\|', l)), None)
assert line20, 'NDEBT-020 row not found'
assert 'rm -f' in line20, 'NDEBT-020 row missing rm -f mention'
assert ('cwd' in line20.lower()) or ('working directory' in line20.lower()), 'NDEBT-020 row missing cwd/working-directory mention'
print('PASS')
"

verification.acceptance_test: .agent IMMUTABILITY GUARD (house two-part invariant: tracked-history-clean + untracked-additions-confined-to-glob, mirroring the 038-amendment_2/041 precedent; REVISED at revision 1 per Mode A round-0 BLOCKER -- see design_notes.entry14_fix): no existing tracked .agent file is modified other than the planner's own pre-existing .agent/feature_list_005.json edit AND this contract's own primary deliverable .agent/product_spec_005.md (scope.files_modify[0], which every correct implementation must modify); no new untracked .agent path exists outside this feature's own contract/qa/evidence artifacts and the planner's own planning_notes_046.md.
verification.command:
tracked_bad=0
while IFS= read -r path; do
  [ -z "$path" ] && continue
  case "$path" in
    .agent/feature_list_005.json|.agent/product_spec_005.md) ;;
    *) echo "out-of-scope tracked .agent modification: $path"; tracked_bad=1 ;;
  esac
done <<< "$(git diff --name-only HEAD -- .agent)"
[ "$tracked_bad" -eq 0 ] || exit 1

untracked_bad=0
while IFS= read -r line; do
  [ -z "$line" ] && continue
  case "$line" in
    '?? '*) ;;
    *) continue ;;
  esac
  path="${line:3}"
  case "$path" in
    .agent/contracts/046.json|.agent/qa/046*.json|.agent/evidence/046-*|.agent/evidence/046/*|.agent/planning_notes_046.md) ;;
    *) echo "out-of-scope untracked .agent artifact: $path"; untracked_bad=1 ;;
  esac
done <<< "$(git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all -- .agent)"
[ "$untracked_bad" -eq 0 ] && echo PASS || exit 1

verification.acceptance_test: SCOPE GUARD (this contract's own vlib_scope_guard-based check, .agent-excluding by design and dry-run proven clean against the current tree; paired with a non-blocking note on the mandated HEAD-caret..HEAD literal's post-commit timing -- see design_notes.head_caret_timing_note): only the 3 contracted files (plus the pre-existing tolerated v0.6.0-release-notes.md) are changed outside .agent/.
verification.command:
source tools/verify_lib.sh
vlib_scope_guard ".agent/product_spec_005.md" "docs/planning/ROADMAP.md" "docs/planning/DEBT.md" "v0.6.0-release-notes.md" || { echo 'vlib_scope_guard reported out-of-scope changed path(s)'; exit 1; }
echo "NOTE (non-blocking, pre-commit): the mandated HEAD-caret..HEAD literal check is timing-deferred until the orchestrator commits feature 046 as the new tip -- see design_notes.head_caret_timing_note"
echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: NO ORCHESTRATOR-STATE MUTATION: .agent/run_state.json is byte-identical to HEAD.
verification.command: git diff --quiet HEAD -- .agent/run_state.json && echo PASS || { echo '.agent/run_state.json was modified -- orchestrator-owned, forbidden'; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: v0.6.0-release-notes.md UNTOUCHED: the pre-existing untracked file's content hash is unchanged from its pinned pre-image.
verification.command:
if [ -f v0.6.0-release-notes.md ]; then
  h=$(sha256sum v0.6.0-release-notes.md | awk '{print $1}')
  [ "$h" = "15ac6acaa9a549a171b5f49617654cac189322fbab760d78104b4c3f4662c00b" ] || { echo "v0.6.0-release-notes.md hash changed: $h"; exit 1; }
fi
echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: CROSS-FEATURE INVARIANT -- 045's RELEASE-READINESS CHECKLIST SHA STILL MATCHES (reused verbatim from contract 045's own entry 17, exit-status-explicit): the SHA-pinned checklist is untouched by this feature.

CROSS-FEATURE INVARIANT -- 045's ZERO-OPEN-P0/P1 DEBT SCAN STILL GREEN (reused verbatim from contract 045's own entry 18, now re-run against the DEBT.md table INCLUDING this feature's own new rows): zero Open High/Critical rows anywhere in the Open section.

DEFAULT COMPLIANCE SWEEP GREEN, EXIT STATUS ASSERTED (feature_list_005.json's own mandated literal, run verbatim -- this IS the NDEBT-019 lesson modeled correctly): validate.sh's own exit code is captured and asserted, not only its piped SUMMARY line.
verification.command: out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; test $rc -eq 0 && echo "$out" | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: 12 passed, 0 failed' && echo PASS || { echo "default sweep failed (rc=$rc)"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: PAYLOAD COMPLIANCE SWEEP GREEN, EXIT STATUS ASSERTED (CORRECTED from feature_list_005.json's own acceptance test 15 -- see design_notes.payload_literal_discrepancy for the discovered discrepancy and why the literal is corrected rather than reproduced verbatim): validate.sh --payload's own exit code is captured and asserted against the real, unchanging payload-mode SUMMARY format.
verification.command: out=$(bash tools/validate.sh --payload 2>&1); rc=$?; test $rc -eq 0 && echo "$out" | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY \(payload mode\): 10 passed, 0 failed' && echo PASS || { echo "payload sweep failed (rc=$rc)"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: DEBT.md ROW-COUNT-DELTA BOUND (NEW at revision 2, companion to entry 10, per @evaluator round-1 LOW finding -- see design_notes.row_count_delta_fix; HEAD-anchored): the total number of '| NDEBT-' rows in the working tree is exactly 2 more than at HEAD, closing the unauthorized-third-row scope-creep vector entry 10's closed-set ID check structurally cannot detect.
verification.command:
head_count=$(git show HEAD:docs/planning/DEBT.md | grep -c '^| NDEBT-')
work_count=$(grep -c '^| NDEBT-' docs/planning/DEBT.md)
delta=$((work_count - head_count))
[ "$delta" -eq 2 ] || { echo "expected exactly 2 new NDEBT rows (HEAD had $head_count, working tree has $work_count, delta=$delta)"; exit 1; }
echo PASS

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured terminal output is written to .agent/evidence/046-verify-NN.txt, where NN is the 1-based index of the command in the 'verification' array, zero-padded to 2 digits, in array order (the 'verification' array has exactly 22 entries -- REVISED at revision 2, entry 22 appended per design_notes.row_count_delta_fix -- so .agent/evidence/046-verify-01.txt through .agent/evidence/046-verify-22.txt). Every evidence file MUST comply with methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md Sec 5's Evidence Capture Convention: the exact invocation as the FIRST line, captured output next, a literal EXIT:<code> as the LAST line, replayable from the repository root, with NO session-absolute paths. One file per command; no inline proof output in this contract or in run_state.json.

approvals.round0_disposition: REJECTED at revision 0 by Mode A. One BLOCKER: verification entry 14's .agent immutability guard whitelisted only .agent/feature_list_005.json in its tracked-diff tolerance, omitting this contract's own primary deliverable .agent/product_spec_005.md (scope.files_modify[0]), which every correct implementation of entries 2/3/4 must modify -- making entry 14 internally contradictory and unsatisfiable by any correct implementation (empirically reproduced by Mode A in a scratch repository: post-implementation, entry 14 exited 1 on the spec file). This also flagged revision-0's dry_run_proof_summary(4) claim of having proven the guard 'both directions' as an unsupported claim, since that proof never actually exercised a fixture modifying the spec deliverable itself. One minor finding: entry 15's non-blocking note referenced a nonexistent design_notes key ('scope_guard_timing_note' instead of the real 'head_caret_timing_note'). FIX (this revision): entry 14's tolerated case extended to '.agent/feature_list_005.json|.agent/product_spec_005.md) ;;'; entry 15's note corrected to reference 'head_caret_timing_note'; dry_run_proof_summary(4) rewritten to describe the corrected, fully-exercised proof (both required polarities: compliant post-implementation state PASSES, unauthorized tracked .agent edit to contracts/044.json FAILS); a new design_notes key entry14_fix records the finding and fix in full. status remains 'proposed'; scope, non_goals, and estimated_lines (14) are unchanged; entries 1-13 and 16-21 are byte-identical to revision 0.
approvals.round1_disposition: REJECTED at revision 1 (evaluator's own round 0 review). The revision-1 entry-14 BLOCKER fix was independently re-confirmed genuinely closed in both required directions. Two NEW findings: (1) HIGH -- entry 9 (this contract's own ROADMAP content-consistency check) AND entry 5 (the feature-list's own mandated stale-phrase-absence literal) were BOTH simultaneously bypassed by a single, realistic meaning-reversing paraphrase ('Track 2 is NOT candidate scope for a subsequent phase; it remains the recommended selection for phase 005 going forward...') that dodges the banned substring while re-asserting the exact stale claim this feature exists to fix -- the NDEBT-014 item-5 paraphrase-mirroring lesson recurring. (2) LOW -- entry 10's closed-set ID check cannot detect an unauthorized third DEBT row added alongside the two authorized ones. FIX (this revision): entry 9 rebuilt to require the EXACT, pinned replacement sentence (now specified verbatim in scope.files_modify[1].purpose, mirroring the 045 checklist-SHA precedent) as a contiguous, line-join-tolerant positive assertion, PLUS an independent remainder co-occurrence guard (no OTHER sentence in the section may co-mention 'Track 2' and 'recommended') as a second, defense-in-depth layer; a new entry 22 (appended, not inserted, to keep entries 1-21 stable) adds a HEAD-anchored row-count-delta bound (working-tree NDEBT-row count minus HEAD count must equal exactly 2) as entry 10's companion. Both fixes re-proven in every required direction (the evaluator's exact meaning-reversal fixture now FAILS; a belt-and-suspenders fixture proves the second layer is independently load-bearing; the compliant fixture PASSES; the real pre-implementation tree still correctly FAILS; the third-row fixture now FAILS entry 22; the compliant two-row fixture PASSES entry 22; entry 14's polarities show no regression). status remains 'proposed'; scope.files_modify[0]/[2], non_goals, and estimated_lines (14) are unchanged; scope.files_modify[1]'s purpose is expanded to carry the pinned exact sentence text, which is a specification clarification required by the fix, not scope growth.
